patches_submitted.subject: [PATCH 2/2] btrfs: test qgroup deletion races with squota writes

version_history.subject: [PATCH 2/2] btrfs: test qgroup deletion races with squota writes

patches_submitted.patch_summary: This patch adds four test cases to the Btrfs file system to cover various race conditions between qgroup deletion and squota writes. The tests aim to identify potential bugs in the handling of extent ownership references (EXTENT_OWNER_REF) when a subvolume is deleted or its qgroup is destroyed. The scenarios tested include freeing an extent whose owner qgroup is gone, creating an extent while the owning subvolume is being deleted, destroying a live subvolume's qgroup with delayed refs, and destroying a qgroup belonging to a still-mounted subvolume. The patch ensures that these operations are properly handled by the Btrfs kernel to prevent underflows or transaction aborts.

patches_submitted.subject: [PATCH 1/2] btrfs: inline enable_quota helper in test 301

version_history.subject: [PATCH 1/2] btrfs: inline enable_quota helper in test 301

patches_submitted.patch_summary: This patch inlines the enable_quota helper function in test 301, removing an unnecessary dispatch and indirection to call 'btrfs quota enable --simple' directly.

review_comments.author: Zorro Lang
review_comments.summary: Expressed neutrality towards the patch, suggesting it be reviewed separately from patch 2/2. Did not provide any technical feedback or opinions on the change itself.
review_comments.raw_body:
On Tue, May 12, 2026 at 05:43:33PM -0700, Boris Burkov wrote:
> The enable_quota helper in btrfs/301 takes a mode argument ('n', 's',
> or anything else) to switch between "no quota", "simple quota", and
> "full quota" enable.  Only the simple-quota path is ever exercised by
> this test, so the dispatch is dead weight and the indirection just
> obscures the actual btrfs command being run.
> 
> Inline the two call sites to call 'btrfs quota enable --simple'
> directly and drop the helper.
> 
> Signed-off-by: Boris Burkov <boris@bur.io>
> ---

Hahah, to be honest, the title '[PATCH 0/2] squota delete fstests' gave me
a minor heart attack. I thought it was a critical bug report about fstests
accidentally deleting itself while running :-D

>  tests/btrfs/301 | 14 ++------------
>  1 file changed, 2 insertions(+), 12 deletions(-)
> 
> diff --git a/tests/btrfs/301 b/tests/btrfs/301
> index 1f72a97b..31243aab 100755
> --- a/tests/btrfs/301
> +++ b/tests/btrfs/301
> @@ -169,16 +169,6 @@ do_enospc_falloc()
>  	do_falloc $file $sz
>  }
>  
> -enable_quota()
> -{
> -	local mode=$1
> -
> -	[ $mode == "n" ] && return
> -	arg=$([ $mode == "s" ] && echo "--simple")
> -
> -	$BTRFS_UTIL_PROG quota enable $arg $SCRATCH_MNT
> -}
> -
>  get_subvid()
>  {
>  	_btrfs_get_subvolid $SCRATCH_MNT subv
> @@ -198,7 +188,7 @@ prepare()
>  {
>  	_scratch_mkfs >> $seqres.full
>  	_scratch_mount
> -	enable_quota "s"
> +	$BTRFS_UTIL_PROG quota enable --simple $SCRATCH_MNT
>  	$BTRFS_UTIL_PROG subvolume create $subv >> $seqres.full
>  	local subvid=$(get_subvid)
>  	set_subvol_limit $subvid $limit
> @@ -421,7 +411,7 @@ enable_mature()
>  	# Sync before enabling squotas to reliably *not* count the writes
>  	# we did before enabling.
>  	sync
> -	enable_quota "s"
> +	$BTRFS_UTIL_PROG quota enable --simple $SCRATCH_MNT

This change doesn't seem related to patch 2/2. I think we can review
these 2 patches separately.

For this patch, I'm fine with or without it. If the btrfs list prefers
to have it, or move it to be a common helper, I'm good with that.

Thanks,
Zorro

>  	set_subvol_limit $subvid $limit
>  	_scratch_cycle_mount
>  	usage=$(get_subvol_usage $subvid)
> -- 
> 2.54.0
> 
>

patches_submitted.subject: [PATCH 0/2] squota delete fstests
patches_submitted.patch_summary: The patch series resurrects a previous attempt to land tests exercising edge cases of squota qgroup deletion, incorporating additional testing and feedback.

review_comments.author: Zorro Lang
review_comments.summary: Expressed concern that the first patch doesn't seem related to the second one and suggested reviewing them separately. Also, mentioned that they're fine with or without the first patch.
review_comments.raw_body:
On Tue, May 12, 2026 at 05:43:33PM -0700, Boris Burkov wrote:
> The enable_quota helper in btrfs/301 takes a mode argument ('n', 's',
> or anything else) to switch between "no quota", "simple quota", and

Hahah, to be honest, the title '[PATCH 0/2] squota delete fstests' gave me

> -

> -
>  get_subvid()
>  {
>  	_btrfs_get_subvolid $SCRATCH_MNT subv
> @@ -198,7 +188,7 @@ prepare()

> -	enable_quota "s"

>  	set_subvol_limit $subvid $limit
> @@ -421,7 +411,7 @@ enable_mature()
>  	# Sync before enabling squotas to reliably *not* count the writes
>  	# we did before enabling.
>  	sync
> -	enable_quota "s"
> +	$BTRFS_UTIL_PROG quota enable --simple $SCRATCH_MNT

This change doesn't seem related to patch 2/2. I think we can review
these 2 patches separately.

For this patch, I'm fine with or without it. If the btrfs list prefers
to have it, or move it to be a common helper, I'm good with that.

Thanks,
Zorro

>  	set_subvol_limit $subvid $limit
>  	_scratch_cycle_mount
>  	usage=$(get_subvol_usage $subvid)
> -- 
> 2.54.0

review_comments.author: Boris Burkov
review_comments.summary: Acknowledged Zorro's concern and explained that the patches were sent together due to context, but can be reviewed separately.
review_comments.raw_body:
On Thu, May 14, 2026 at 11:34:00AM +0800, Zorro Lang wrote:
> On Tue, May 12, 2026 at 05:43:33PM -0700, Boris Burkov wrote:
> > The enable_quota helper in btrfs/301 takes a mode argument ('n', 's',
> > or anything else) to switch between "no quota", "simple quota", and

> Hahah, to be honest, the title '[PATCH 0/2] squota delete fstests' gave me

> accidentally deleting itself while running :-D
> 

Oops, sorry!

> > -

> > -}
> > -
> >  get_subvid() > >  {
> >  	_btrfs_get_subvolid $SCRATCH_MNT subv
> > @@ -198,7 +188,7 @@ prepare()
> >  {
> >  	_scratch_mkfs >> $seqres.full
> >  	_scratch_mount
> > -	enable_quota "s"
> > +	$BTRFS_UTIL_PROG quota enable --simple $SCRATCH_MNT

> >  	set_subvol_limit $subvid $limit

> >  	# Sync before enabling squotas to reliably *not* count the writes

I pulled this into a separate patch based on Filipe's feedback on the
old version I failed to get in. Agreed it doesn't need to be a series,
just felt related enough to send in a batch, due to the context.

Thanks for taking a look,
Boris

> >  	set_subvol_limit $subvid $limit
> >  	_scratch_cycle_mount
> >  	usage=$(get_subvol_usage $subvid)
> > -- 
> > 2.54.0
